$comment: Canonical home: https://jseverino.com/schemas/cordon-checks-v1.json (the value of $id above) — the live, immutable URL this schema_version pins. The copy in the cordon repo at schema/cordon-checks-v1.json is the byte-identical development mirror; reference it for validation. The repo-level sibling of cordon-v4.json: where the command-surface contract answers 'what does running this command cost?', this answers 'is this repo shippable, and what fixes each failure?'. checks/run.mjs --json is the reference emitter.
title: Cordon checks verdict (schema_version 1)

ok.$comment: The gate: true iff no check failed. Unlike the command-surface contract, this is a real verdict, not a const sanity marker.

failed.$comment: ids of the failed checks — the same ids that appear in checks[].id with status 'fail'.

report.$comment: Relative path to the written failure report, or null when ok.

durationMs.$comment: The one runtime field — a verdict is a report, not a static description, so wall-clock cost is legitimately part of it. Everything else is deterministic: checks ride in registry order, failed[] is derived.

effect.$comment: The check's own blast radius on cordon's ladder — the cost of producing this row, in the same vocabulary a command's --describe uses.

fix.$comment: Machine-readable remediation. The verdict-side mirror of effect: effect is the risk read before acting, fix is the remediation read after a failure. Required on a failed check.

rerun.$comment: The exact, deterministic command that reproduces this one check standalone. Required on a failed check.

then.$comment: A failed check must carry its remediation: machine-readable fix + the deterministic command that reproduces it. The property shapes are defined on the check object above; they are restated here only so the requirement is self-contained under strict validation.
